pattern: A single-tab browser's history is just a list with a cursor: visiting a new page truncates everything ahead of the cursor before appending, and back/forward simply clamp the cursor's movement to the list's bounds.
hints: Keep every visited page in one growing list plus an integer cursor pointing at the current page; forward history is just 'everything in the list after the cursor'.
visit(url) must discard all entries after the cursor before appending url, so any forward history is permanently gone the moment a new page is visited.
back(steps) and forward(steps) move the cursor by steps but clamp it to stay within [0, list.length - 1]; both return the URL now sitting at the cursor.

edge_inputs.description: visit a few pages then walk back and forward within range
visiting after going back truncates the forward history entirely
back/forward with steps larger than the entire history clamp to the two ends
no visits at all: back and forward from the homepage do nothing
forward immediately after a fresh visit (no prior back) must not move at all since there is no forward history
repeated back calls that overshoot the start must settle on the homepage, not error or wrap

stress.description: long randomized mix of visit, back, and forward with small step counts
stress.note: small step counts keep back/forward frequently landing mid-history rather than always saturating at an end
